name: okpack-threat-actors-feed-fetch
_note: Enabled by default but HARMLESS until you populate feeds.opml: feed-fetch runs on schedule and makes ZERO upstream calls while the feed list is empty (it logs a clean 'no feeds' no-op). Populate feeds/feeds.opml to start ingesting. The `@jitter:2h` schedule is expanded to a concrete cron with a RANDOM minute at install (framework init/pull), so installs don't synchronize once feeds are live.
prompt: okpack-threat-actors feed pull: fetch the threat-actors OPML into raw/threat-actors/ for the ingest lane. Pure script, wakeAgent=false.

name: okpack-threat-actors-attack-import
_note: THE graph seed. Pulls MITRE ATT&CK (Enterprise+Mobile+ICS STIX) into actor/campaign/malware/tool/technique + mitigation pages with alias arrays + relationship links. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens. MERGE-writes, so it never clobbers attribution_confidence/related_actors from the enrich+correlation lanes. Weekly is ample (ATT&CK releases ~quarterly).
prompt: Seed the adversary graph from MITRE ATT&CK (Enterprise+Mobile+ICS). Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-authority-review-reconcile
_note: Claim-scoped review reconciliation for existing actor pages backed by locally stored primary-authority advisories. Requires an exact approved host/path, actor-name match, confirmed attribution, associated source ref, and no conflicts. News-only records remain review-gated. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Reconcile actor review state from claim-matched primary-authority source pages. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-misp-import
_note: The alias engine. Unions the MISP threat-actor galaxy's synonyms onto the ATT&CK-seeded actors (matching by name/alias) and creates low-trust pages for galaxy actors ATT&CK misses. Runs after attack-import so it enriches. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Enrich actor aliases from the MISP threat-actor galaxy. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-correlation
_note: Rarity-weighted (idf) actor<->actor correlation from shared techniques/software/sectors — shared Cobalt Strike ~= 0, shared rare custom malware = strong. Writes low-trust related_actors leads (needs_review). Reads the structured frontmatter attack_import stamps, so it wants the imports to have run at least once. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Correlate actors by rarity-weighted shared tradecraft. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-signature-ttps
_note: Per-actor SIGNATURE techniques — the rarest (high-idf) TTPs few other actors use (the detection-engineering distinctive set), + a distinctiveness_score. Inverse of correlation. Reads the structured frontmatter attack_import stamps. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Profile each actor's signature (rarest) techniques. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-shared-tooling
_note: Classifies malware/tools proprietary vs shared vs commodity by how many actors use them, and links actors that share PROPRIETARY tooling (a strong same-group / RaaS-affiliate disambiguation signal — commodity/RaaS tooling is discounted). no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Classify shared tooling + link actors sharing proprietary malware. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-aptnotes-seed
_note: HISTORICAL seed: ~689 public vendor APT reports (2006+) from the APTnotes index as source pages, with titles matched to actor aliases -> [[actor]] links. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens, idempotent. APTnotes is a mostly-static corpus, so this seeds once and re-runs are cheap no-ops — safe to disable after the first run.
prompt: Seed historical APT reports from the APTnotes index. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-annual-reports
_note: Full-TEXT ingest of annual security reports (awesome-annual-security-reports, MIT). THREAT categories only (keyword allowlist). Default: fetch from the PUBLIC GitHub repo, bounded (recent years + cap 80). Operator override: set ANNUAL_REPORTS_DIR in .env to a local checkout (faster/offline). Widen via ANNUAL_REPORTS_YEARS / _LIMIT. no_agent for the import (zero-token; actor-linking too) — but these are large SOURCE pages, so the entity/trend/concept AGENT lanes spend when they compile them. See docs/data-sources.md.
prompt: Ingest annual threat reports (full text) from the awesome-annual-security-reports corpus. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens for the import.

name: okpack-threat-actors-actor-activity
_note: Generated dashboard: top actors by RECENT activity (mentions in recent reporting). no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens. Reads source mentions_actors+published stamped by the ingest lanes.
prompt: Rank actors by recent reporting activity into a dashboard. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-cti-dashboards
_note: Generated CTI research dashboards (actors-by-sector, ATT&CK tactic-coverage, top exploited-CVEs, most-used tooling). no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Generate CTI research dashboards. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-cve-actor-edge
_note: Materializes the actor<->CVE pivot: stamps exploited_cve_ids on actor pages and exploiting_actors on cve pages (direct actor CVE fields + the actor->technique->exploits_cve seam), so the cockpit Adversaries/Vulnerabilities tabs can drill the relationship both ways. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Materialize the actor<->CVE edge (exploited_cve_ids / exploiting_actors). Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-weekly-brief
_note: Agent lane. Synthesizes the week's threat brief from the last 7 days. Spends model tokens (compile budget). Writes briefings/weekly-<date>.md (weekly- prefix keeps it distinct from the engine daily-brief's daily-<date>).
prompt: Weekly threat-actor brief. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call: file_read on wiki/HOT.md (recent sources, recently-updated actors/malware, open forecasts). Scope to the LAST 7 DAYS only — ignore older HOT.md entries. Then write the brief via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity (the enforced write path — never file_write) to path "briefings/weekly-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md" (frontmatter: type: briefing, title, published: <YYYY-MM-DD>, updated: <YYYY-MM-DD> — use TODAY's actual date, not a guessed or future one, for the filename and both date fields) covering the 4-6 most significant developments of the week — active campaigns, notable new/updated actors, TTP shifts, credible attribution moves, active malware — each 2-3 sentences, and EVERY item ends with a "Source: [[sources/...]]" line citing the source page it came from. Entity links are GROUNDED links: only wikilink an entity the cited source actually names; a related-but-unnamed entity may appear ONLY as an explicit "Related (not named in this report): [[...]]" suffix. Note any open forecast that moved, and any newly correlated actors worth an analyst's eye. Treat vendor attribution as a CLAIM, not fact. Expert-grade and terse; skip 101 explanation. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.

name: okpack-threat-actors-msft-import
_note: The cross-vendor ALIAS BACKBONE. Unions Microsoft's weather-suffix actor names + their 'Other names' (github.com/microsoft/mstic, MIT) onto the ATT&CK/MISP-seeded actor pages so a feed mention under any vendor's naming resolves to one canonical actor. Runs AFTER attack_import + misp_galaxy_import (enrich, not race). no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens, MERGE-safe. The mapping changes infrequently -> weekly.
prompt: Enrich actor aliases from the Microsoft threat-actor naming map. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-url-reconcile
prompt: Deterministic url repair: re-join source pages to their raw feed items by exact title and overwrite model-retyped urls with the feed's REAL url (observed ~20% mutation rate on retyped URLs). Runs 40min past even hours — after the feed-fetch (:08/:26/:32) and ingest lanes. Pure script, wakeAgent=false.

name: okpack-threat-actors-actor-news-activity
_note: Stamps news_last_seen + recent_news onto actor pages by matching the roster's names/aliases against the NEWS firehose (source_kind:news) — the recency mentions_actors (report-only) can't provide. Feeds the cockpit 'Recently active' board. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens. Daily.
prompt: Stamp per-actor news recency (news_last_seen + recent_news) from the news firehose. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-actors-origin-country-normalize
_note: Converges imported actor origin_country values to ISO 3166-1 alpha-2. Ambiguous descriptions remain untouched for review.
prompt: Normalize actor origin-country values to canonical ISO alpha-2 codes. Pure script, wakeAgent=false.
